brand_focus: Cooking tips, recipes, and marinades with AI-styled ingredient photography. Audience is Europe-first, US a strong second. Goal: grow followers fast-but-safe by making every post highly shareable (sent to a friend) and saveable.

slots.note: Tue 18:30 CET — EU dinner-prep window; also = ~12:30 US Eastern lunch (dual EU/US coverage).
Wed lunch — Wed is the strongest food day; carousel = save-bait recipe for EU meal-planning.
Thu 19:00 CET — EU evening; also = ~13:00 US Eastern lunch (dual coverage).
Sat late-morning — EU weekend brunch / what-to-cook intent.

content_plan.marinade: One complete marinade: ingredients with memorable ratios, what it's best on, and exactly how long to marinate (food-safe times). Built to be saved.
content_plan.quick-dinner: A genuinely fast (~30 min) weeknight recipe with a short ingredient list. As a carousel: ingredients -> key step -> finished dish. End on 'save this for a busy night'.
content_plan.technique: One practical technique or kitchen tip in a few clear steps. Make it the kind of thing people send to a friend who'd find it useful.
content_plan.weekend-cook: A slightly more ambitious recipe worth the weekend time — the 'impressive but doable' post.
content_plan.flavor: A flavor pairing or single-spice spotlight: what it tastes like and three ways to use it.
content_plan.sauce: A versatile sauce, dressing, or glaze with ratios people can memorize.
content_plan.pantry: An ingredient spotlight or a smart prep / pantry tip.

caption_strategy: Open with a keyword-rich first line someone would actually search ('garlic honey chicken marinade, ready in 10 minutes') — Instagram search/SEO now reads captions. Keep the body skimmable. Because the audience is Europe-first with a strong US second, give measurements in metric (g/ml) with US cups/oz in parentheses where it helps, and keep ingredients widely available on both sides of the Atlantic. Always include one explicit beat that drives the top growth signals: 'save this for later' and/or 'send this to whoever cooks dinner'.
hashtag_strategy: Use 3-5 highly relevant hashtags, chosen fresh per post (never a reused block). Favor mid-size niche tags (~10K-500K posts) over mega-tags. Mix one or two niche-community tags, one format tag, and one topic tag matching the dish.
learnings: COLD START (first weeks, ~0 followers, little data): the algorithm auditions every post against non-followers, so follower count is irrelevant — optimize 100% for shareability and saves. Lead with the finished dish / strongest visual. Keep captions search-friendly. Audience is Europe-first, US strong second: the evening EU slots (Tue/Thu) deliberately double as US-midday, so keep topics and ingredients trans-Atlantic. Stay consistent on the 4-slot slate; don't blast volume early. As real Insights accumulate, this note gets rewritten toward what actually performs — including shifting times if your followers skew to one region.
